resources.string: Are you sure you want to delete this server? (This only deletes the server from the app, it does not delete the server files)
Path to the server installation folder, changing this will not move the existing installation.
Enables Ark Ascended Server API, which allows the use of plugins. These are not the same as mods. Requires reinstall.
The password users must enter in order to access the server. If this is set, only people who know the password can access your server.
The password users must enter to execute admin/cheat commands on the server. If this is not set, anyone can use cheats.
The port users will use to connect to your server. Default is 7777. Change this and Query Port if you wish to host multiple servers on one machine.
Peer port (always Game port +1), used for connecting to the server from the client.
The port Steam uses to communicate with your server. Default is 27015. Change this and Server Port if you wish to host multiple servers on one machine.
Enable RCON (Remote Console) for this server. This allows you to execute admin/cheat commands on the server.
The port that RCON will use on your server.
The map your server will load. Make sure to use the correct map name, otherwise your server might not start.
A comma-separated list of mod ids, in the order in which they should be applied.
Set how often the server will automatically save the game. Setting it to a very low value may impact performance, setting this to 0 will cause constant saving.
This message will be shown to players as a welcome message when they join the server.
Set how long the Message of the Day will be shown to players.
If enabled, this will allow downloading external survivors, items, and dinos into your server.
If enabled, this will prevent downloading survivors from the server.
If enabled, this will prevent downloading items from the server.
If enabled, this will prevent downloading dinosaurs from the server.
If enabled, this allows non-native creatures tribute downloads on Aberration.
If enabled, this will prevent uploading survivors.
If enabled, this will prevent uploading items.
If enabled, this will prevent uploading dinosaurs.
Prevents ARK Data usage between single player and servers that do not have a cluster ID. Even with a cluster ID set, keep this enabled to fully disable code paths that allow it.
Limits number of dinosaurs that can be uploaded in a tribute. Default is 20. Range is 20-100.
Limits number of items that can be uploaded in a tribute. Default is 50. Range is 50-100.

Enable to write tribute character expiration in minutes. Warning: do not set this option to an extremely high value; it can overflow and may cause upload time checks to fail and ARK Data to be deleted.

Set, in minutes, the expiration timer for uploaded dinosaurs in ARK Data. If set to 0 or lower, the server will revert to default. Warning: do not set this option to an extremely high value; it can overflow and may cause upload time checks to fail and ARK Data to be deleted.

Set, in minutes, the expiration timer for uploaded items in ARK Data. If set to 0 or lower, the server will revert to default. Warning: do not set this option to an extremely high value; it can overflow and may cause upload time checks to fail and ARK Data to be deleted.
Sets the maximum number of players which can join this server.
Determines how many lines of game logs are send over the RCON.
If enabled, idle players will be kicked from the server after a specified amount of time.
The amount of time (in seconds) after which idle players will be kicked from the server. This option is only relevant if idle player kick is enabled. (3600 seconds is one hour)
Ban list URL, sets the global ban list.
Enables the use of the dynamic config
Direct link to the live tuning file.
Select the root folder of the server installation:
The root folder of the server installation. You can identify the installation path by looking for the 'ShooterGame' and 'Engine' folder in the server installation directory.
The selected path is not a valid server installation.
SteamCMD path not configured in settings.
Cluster ID is automatically set based on the selected cluster. (This field is used by ark to group servers together)
The location where cluster data like player and item uploads are stored (This field is used by ark to group servers together)
Override the server locale. This does not affect the client locale.
Disable BattlEye on the server. This will also disable BattlEye on the client.
Disables the Custom Cosmetic system (allowing players to use and display special mods that should only be skins and will be downloaded automatically by the connected clients).
Launch with this command to destroy all wild creatures on server start-up. Note: this will only destroy wild creatures that are not currently tamed.

Prevents wild creatures from being spawned. Enabling this option will make the following options unusable: -NoDinosExceptForcedSpawn, -NoDinosExceptStreamingSpawn, -NoDinosExceptManualSpawn and -NoDinosExceptWaterSpawn.
Chance for Easter colors when creatures spawn.
Specifies the scaling factor for creature taming speed. Higher values make taming faster.

Specifies the scaling factor for yields from all harvesting activities (chopping down trees, picking berries, carving carcasses, mining rocks, etc.). Higher values increase the amount of materials harvested with each strike.

Specifies the scaling factor for the "health" of items that can be harvested (trees, rocks, carcasses, etc.). Higher values increase the amount of damage (i.e., "number of strikes") such objects can withstand before being destroyed, which results in higher overall harvest yields.
Specifies the scaling factor for the re-spawn rate for resource nodes (trees, rocks, bushes, etc.). Lower values cause nodes to re-spawn more frequently.

Controls how resources regrow closer or farther away from players. Values higher than 1.0 increase the distance around players where resources are not allowed to grow back. Values between 0 and 1.0 will reduce it.

Controls how resources regrow closer or farther away from structures. Values higher than 1.0 increase the distance around structures where resources are not allowed to grow back. Values between 0 and 1.0 will reduce it.
Specifies the map base temperature scaling factor: lower value makes the environment colder, higher value makes the environment hotter.

Specifies the scaling factor for the passage of time in the ARK, controlling how often day changes to night and night changes to day. The default value 1 provides the same cycle speed as the single player experience (and the official public servers). Values lower than 1 slow down the cycle; higher values accelerate it. Base time when value is 1 appears to be 1-minute real time equals approx. 28-minutes game time. Thus, for an approximate 24-hour day/night cycle in game, use .035 for the value.

Specifies the scaling factor for the passage of time in the ARK during the day. This value determines the length of each day, relative to the length of each night (as specified by NightTimeSpeedScale). Lowering this value increases the length of each day.

Specifies the scaling factor for the passage of time in the ARK during the night. This value determines the length of each night, relative to the length of each day (as specified by DayTimeSpeedScale). Lowering this value increases the length of each night.
Scales the spoiling time of perishables globally. Higher values prolong the time.
Scales the decomposition time of dropped items, loot bags etc. globally. Higher values prolong the time.
Scales the decomposition time of corpses, (player and creature), globally. Higher values prolong the time.
Scales the speed of crop decay in plots. A higher value decrease (by percentage) speed of crop decay in plots.
Scales the speed of crop growth in plots. A higher value increases (by percentage) speed of crop growth.
Scales the time between eggs are spawning / being laid. Higher number increases it (by percentage).
Scales how frequently survivors can poop. Higher value decreases it (by percentage).
Scales the hair growth. Higher values increase speed of growth.
Scales the amount of XP earned for generic XP (automatic over time).
Overrides items crafting resource requirements. See Item related section for more details.
Scales the amount of XP earned for harvesting.
Scale the amount of XP earned for a kill.
Scale the amount of XP earned for SpecialEvent.

If enabled, limit the damage caused by a tame to a resource on harvesting based on resource remaining health. Note: enabling this setting may result in sensible resource harvesting reduction using high damage tools or creatures.

If enabled, clamps all spoiling times to the items' maximum spoiling times. Useful if any infinite-spoiling exploits were used on the server and you wish to clean them up. Could potentially cause issues with mods that alter spoiling time, hence it is an option.
Automatically restart the server if it crashes, this is done within 5 seconds of the server crashing.
Enables Hardcore mode (player characters revert to level 1 upon death)
If enabled prevents Friendly-Fire in PvP (among tribe mates/tames/structures)
If enabled prevents Friendly-Fire in PvE (among tribe mates/tames/structures)
Prevent building in resource rich areas
If enabled, disables building in specific resource-rich areas
If enabled, prevents spawning of Loot crates (artifact creates will still spawn)
If enabled, disables PVP and enables PvE
If enabled, allows building in caves when PvE mode is also enabled
If enabled, allows building in caves when PvP mode is also enabled
If Enabled, all game settings will be more balanced for an individual player experience. Useful for dedicated server with a very small amount of players.
If Enabled, adds a button to the pause menu to enable/disable creative mode.
If enabled, enables Cryopod cooldown timer when deploying a creature in PvE.
If enabled, supply drops are in random locations. Note: This setting is known to cause artifacts becoming inaccessible on Ragnarok if active.
Allow Crate Spawns on Top of Structures
If enabled, allows from-the-air Supply Crates to appear on top of Structures, rather than being prevented by Structures
Sets the maximum survivors allowed in a tribe. A value of 1 effectively disables tribes. The default value of 0 means there is no limit.
Cool-down, in minutes, in between tribe name changes. Official servers use a value of 2880 (2 days).

Locks a tribe slot for the value in minutes, e.g.: a value of 60 would mean that if a survivor leaves the tribe, their place cannot be taken by another survivor (or re-join) for 1 hour. Used on Official Small Tribes Servers.
If enabled, allows tribes to officially declare alliances with each other.
If enabled, allows custom RP-oriented Recipe/Cooking System (including Skill-Based results).
Scales the effectiveness of custom recipes. A higher value increases (by percentage) their effectiveness.
Scales the effect of the players crafting speed level that is used as a base for the formula in creating a custom recipe. A higher number increases (by percentage) the effect.
If enabled, completely enables diseases on the server.
If enabled, makes permanent diseases not permanent. Players will lose them if on re-spawn.
If set, defines maximum alliances a tribe can form. A value of 0 means no limit.
If set, defines maximum number of tribes allowed in an alliance. A value of 0 means no limit.
If enabled, tribes can officially declare war on each other for mutually-agreed-upon periods (PvE).
If enabled, allows cancellation of an agreed-upon war before it has started.
Editing these configs can break your server if not done correctly. Make sure you know what you're doing and have a backup of your configs before applying changes. Use this feature at your own risk.
You can also use this feature to add non-managed/custom fields and sections to your configs.
If enabled, allows flying creatures to pick up wild creatures in PvE.
Specifies the scaling factor for the experience received by players, tribes and tames for various actions. Higher values increase XP amounts awarded for various actions.
Specifies the scaling factor for the damage players deal with their attacks. The default value 1 provides normal damage. Higher values increase damage. Lower values decrease it.

Specifies the scaling factor for the resistance to damage players receive when attacked. The default value 1 provides normal damage. Higher values decrease resistance, increasing damage per attack. Lower values increase it, reducing damage per attack.
Specifies the scaling factor for player characters' water consumption. Higher values increase water consumption (player characters get thirsty faster).
Specifies the scaling factor for player characters' food consumption. Higher values increase food consumption (player characters get hungry faster).
Specifies the scaling factor for player characters' stamina consumption. Higher values increase stamina consumption (player characters get tired faster).
Specifies the scaling factor for player characters' health recovery. Higher values increase the recovery rate (player characters heal faster).
Scales the damage done to a harvestable item/entity by a Player. A higher value increases it (by percentage): the higher number, the faster the survivors collects.
Scales the bonus received from upgrading the Crafting Skill.
Defines the falling speed multiplier at which players starts taking fall damage. The higher this setting is, the longer players can fall without taking fall damage.
If enabled, changes player base stats by multiplying default values. This affects start stats for new spawned characters.
Specifies base multiplier for Health. Higher values increase base Health; lower values decrease base Health.
Specifies base multiplier for Stamina. Higher values increase base Stamina; lower values decrease base Stamina.
Specifies base multiplier for Torpidity. Higher values increase base Torpidity; lower values decrease base Torpidity.
Specifies base multiplier for Oxygen. Higher values increase base Oxygen; lower values decrease base Oxygen.
Specifies base multiplier for Food. Higher values increase base Food; lower values decrease base Food.
Specifies base multiplier for Water. Higher values increase base Water; lower values decrease base Water.
Specifies base multiplier for Temperature. Higher values increase base Temperature; lower values decrease base Temperature.
Specifies base multiplier for Weight. Higher values increase base Weight; lower values decrease base Weight.
Specifies base multiplier for Melee Damage. Higher values increase base Melee Damage; lower values decrease base Melee Damage.
Specifies base multiplier for Movement Speed. Higher values increase base Movement Speed; lower values decrease base Movement Speed.
Specifies base multiplier for Fortitude. Higher values increase base Fortitude; lower values decrease base Fortitude.
Specifies base multiplier for Crafting Speed. Higher values increase base Crafting Speed; lower values decrease base Crafting Speed.
If enabled, changes amount of stats gained for each level.
Specifies multiplier for health gained per level. Higher values increase Health faster; lower values increase Health slower.
Specifies multiplier for stamina gained per level. Higher values increase Stamina faster; lower values increase Stamina slower.
Specifies multiplier for torpidity gained per level. Higher values increase Torpidity faster; lower values increase Torpidity slower.
Specifies multiplier for oxygen gained per level. Higher values increase Oxygen faster; lower values increase Oxygen slower.
Specifies multiplier for food gained per level. Higher values increase Food faster; lower values increase Food slower.
Specifies multiplier for water gained per level. Higher values increase Water faster; lower values increase Water slower.
Specifies multiplier for temperature gained per level. Higher values increase Temperature faster; lower values increase Temperature slower.
Specifies multiplier for weight gained per level. Higher values increase Weight faster; lower values increase Weight slower.
Specifies multiplier for melee damage gained per level. Higher values increase Melee Damage faster; lower values increase Melee Damage slower.
Specifies multiplier for movement speed gained per level. Higher values increase Movement Speed faster; lower values increase Movement Speed slower.
Specifies multiplier for fortitude gained per level. Higher values increase Fortitude faster; lower values increase Fortitude slower.
Specifies multiplier for crafting speed gained per level. Higher values increase Crafting Speed faster; lower values increase Crafting Speed slower.

If True, allows Titanosaurs to be permanently tamed (namely allow them to be fed). Note: in The Island only spawns a maximum of 3 Titanosaurs, so 3 tamed ones should ultimately block any more ones from spawning.
Sets a per-tribe creature tame limit (500 on official PvE servers, 300 in official PvP servers). The default value of 0 disables such limit.
Sets the maximum number of tame creatures on a server, this is a global cap. Note: although at code level it is defined as a floating-point number, it is suggested to use an integer instead.
Specifies the scaling factor for creatures\' health recovery. Higher values increase the recovery rate (creatures heal faster).
Specifies the scaling factor for creatures\' stamina consumption. Higher values increase stamina consumption (creatures get tired faster).
Specifies the scaling factor for the damage wild creatures deal with their attacks. The default value 1 provides normal damage. Higher values increase damage. Lower values decrease it.

Specifies the scaling factor for the resistance to damage wild creatures receive when attacked. The default value 1 provides normal damage. Higher values decrease resistance, increasing damage per attack. Lower values increase it, reducing damage per attack.
Specifies the scaling factor for the damage tame creatures deal with their attacks. The default value 1 provides normal damage. Higher values increase damage. Lower values decrease it.

Specifies the scaling factor for the resistance to damage tame creatures receive when attacked. The default value 1 provides normal damage. Higher values decrease resistance, increasing damage per attack. Lower values increase it, reducing damage per attack.
Affects how quickly the food drains on such raid dinos (e.g.: Titanosaurus).
Scales how fast tame creatures consume food.
Scales how fast wild creatures consume food.
Scales how fast tamed creatures lose torpor.
Scales how fast wild creatures lose torpor.
Scales the damage done to a harvestable item/entity by a tame. A higher number increases (by percentage) the speed of harvesting.
Scales the damage done by Turrets towards a creature. A higher value increases it (by percentage).
Scales how often a survivor gets tame requests for passive tame creatures.
If enabled, changes amount of stats gained for each level on tamed creatures.
If enabled, changes the amount immediately added for tamed creatures.
If enabled, changes the amount applied dependant on affinity for tamed creatures.
If enabled, changes amount of stats gained for each level on wild creatures.
Specifies multiplier for health gained per level. Higher values increase Health faster; lower values increase Health slower.
Specifies multiplier for stamina gained per level. Higher values increase Stamina faster; lower values increase Stamina slower.
Specifies multiplier for torpidity gained per level. Higher values increase Torpidity faster; lower values increase Torpidity slower.
Specifies multiplier for oxygen gained per level. Higher values increase Oxygen faster; lower values increase Oxygen slower.
Specifies multiplier for food gained per level. Higher values increase Food faster; lower values increase Food slower.
Specifies multiplier for water gained per level. Higher values increase Water faster; lower values increase Water slower.
Specifies multiplier for temperature gained per level. Higher values increase Temperature faster; lower values increase Temperature slower.
Specifies multiplier for weight gained per level. Higher values increase Weight faster; lower values increase Weight slower.
Specifies multiplier for melee damage gained per level. Higher values increase Melee Damage faster; lower values increase Melee Damage slower.
Specifies multiplier for movement speed gained per level. Higher values increase Movement Speed faster; lower values increase Movement Speed slower.
Specifies multiplier for fortitude gained per level. Higher values increase Fortitude faster; lower values increase Fortitude slower.
Specifies multiplier for crafting speed gained per level. Higher values increase Crafting Speed faster; lower values increase Crafting Speed slower.
